steps.title: Determine the median position
steps.content:
With 25 objects, the median is the 13th value when all heights are arranged in order:
$$\text{median position}=\frac{25+1}{2}=13$$

steps.title: Build cumulative frequency to locate the interval containing the 13th value
steps.content:
Using the histogram data:
  • Heights 0-100 cm: 8 objects; cumulative total 8
  • Heights 101-200 cm: 6 more objects; cumulative total 14
The 13th object falls after cumulative total 8 and by cumulative total 14, so it lies in the 101-200 cm interval.

steps.title: Match to the answer choice
steps.content:
Find which option falls in the 101–200 cm range:
  • A) 355 cm — falls in the 301–400 cm interval. Incorrect.
  • B) 255 cm — falls in the 201–300 cm interval. Incorrect.
  • C) 155 cm — falls in the 101–200 cm interval. C is correct.
  • D) 55 cm — falls in the 0–100 cm interval. Incorrect.
